qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: The primary purpose is to upgrade and renew nearly 80% of the High Performance Computing (HPC) and Cloud services equipment for Canadian researchers, as existing systems were approaching end-of-life.

qa_pairs.question: When did the infrastructure renewal begin and when were new systems made available?
qa_pairs.answer: The renewal began in winter 2024–2025, with new systems becoming available to users throughout 2025 and early 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What improvements do the new systems offer?
qa_pairs.answer: The new systems provide faster processing speeds, greater storage capacity, and improved reliability.

qa_pairs.question: Which new system replaced Béluga?
qa_pairs.answer: Rorqual replaced Béluga.

qa_pairs.question: Which new system replaced Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir replaced Cedar.

qa_pairs.question: Which new system replaced Niagara and Mist?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium replaced Niagara and Mist.

qa_pairs.question: Which new system replaced Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: Nibi replaced Graham.

qa_pairs.question: Why might outages and reductions in service be unavoidable during the transition?
qa_pairs.answer: Outages and reductions are unavoidable due to constraints on space and electrical power during the installation and transition to the new infrastructure.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of the upgraded Arbutus Cloud as of April 7, 2026?
qa_pairs.answer: As of April 7, 2026, the upgraded Arbutus Cloud is in production and available to RAC 2026–2027 recipients.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for migrating existing resources from legacy Arbutus, and by when?
qa_pairs.answer: Both RAC recipients and RAS users with 2025–2026 allocations on legacy Arbutus are responsible for migrating existing resources before August 31, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What are the new authentication requirements for the Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: The new Arbutus Cloud introduces single sign-on (SSO) authentication with multi-factor authentication (MFA), requiring users to select 'Authenticate using Digital Research Alliance of Canada' when logging in.

qa_pairs.question: When is access to all remaining Béluga data scheduled to end?
qa_pairs.answer: Access to all remaining Béluga data will end on June 20, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Are files stored on Cedar and Graham automatically available on their new replacement systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, files stored on Cedar are available on Fir, and files stored on Graham are available on Nibi, as these pairs of clusters share the same file systems, requiring no user action.

qa_pairs.question: How will the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) be impacted by this transition?
qa_pairs.answer: The application process remains the same, but 2025/26 RAC allocations will only become available once all new clusters are in service. Users may also experience a period when both 2024 and 2025 awards are unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more training opportunities?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can discover more training opportunities through Explora (explora.alliancecan.ca).

qa_pairs.question: Will my data be automatically copied to its new system?
qa_pairs.answer: No, data migration to the new systems is the responsibility of each National Host Site, which will inform users of the necessary steps.

qa_pairs.question: Will my files be deleted during a complete data centre shutdown as part of renewal activities?
qa_pairs.answer: No, /project and /home data will be migrated by the National Host Site, and tape systems for backups and /nearline data will remain unchanged.

qa_pairs.question: How will users be informed about outages?
qa_pairs.answer: Specific outages will be described on the system status web page (status.alliancecan.ca), with more general updates on this wiki page and via periodic email notices.

qa_pairs.question: Whom should I contact for questions about the transition?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can contact technical support for questions about the transition.

qa_pairs.question: Will my jobs and applications still run on the new systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Generally yes, but some applications may require recompilation or reconfiguration due to new CPUs and GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: Will commercial, licensed software be migrated to the new systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the plan is to transition current commercial software licenses from old to new systems, aiming for identical access, though there's a small risk of providers changing licensing terms.

qa_pairs.question: Can I purchase old hardware after equipment upgrades?
qa_pairs.answer: Generally no, most equipment is legally the property of the hosting institution and is typically 'e-cycled' (recycled) rather than sold, following the institution's guidelines.
